title: Traffic Light State Machine
prompt_md:
## Goal
Model a traffic light using an unscoped enumeration and report both the current light and the next light in the cycle.

The cycle runs: red -> green -> yellow -> red.

## Input
- A single word naming the current light: `red`, `yellow`, or `green`.

## Output
Two lines:
- Line 1: the name of the current light (`red`, `yellow`, or `green`)
- Line 2: the name of the next light in the cycle

## Constraints
- Represent the lights with an unscoped `enum`.
- Map the input word to an enumerator and compute the next light by working with the enumerators themselves (e.g. a `switch`).
- The input is always one of `red`, `yellow`, or `green`.

title: Days Until Target Weekday
prompt_md:
## Goal
Represent the days of the week with an unscoped enumeration, then compute how many days you must advance forward from a starting day to reach a target day (wrapping around the end of the week). Use the fact that enumerators implicitly convert to `int` to do the arithmetic.

## Input
- Line 1: the starting day name (`Sunday`, `Monday`, ..., `Saturday`)
- Line 2: the target day name (same set of names)

## Output
One line: a single integer in the range 0-6 -- the number of days you advance forward from the start day to reach the target day. If both days are the same, the answer is `0`.

## Constraints
- Use an unscoped `enum` for the days, ordered Sunday (first) through Saturday.
- Map each input word to an enumerator, then rely on the enumerators' implicit conversion to `int` for the subtraction. Do not cast an integer back into the enum type.

test_cases.label: Sample -- Monday to Thursday is 3 days
Hidden -- Saturday to Sunday wraps to 1 day
Hidden -- Sunday to Saturday is 6 days
